title: Fix crash when handling empty adb key list
body: This commit fixes a crash in the ADB module when no keys were parsed. We should return an empty list by default.

step_plan.summary: Replace iteration over parsed.get('user_keys') with parsed.get('user_keys', []) in DumpsysADBArtifact.parse so the parser treats missing/None user_keys as an empty list and no longer crashes.

steps.operation: Guard iteration over parsed user_keys with a default empty list
steps.reason: The parser currently does `for user_key in parsed.get('user_keys'):` which raises a TypeError when parsed.get('user_keys') returns None. Using parsed.get('user_keys', []) ensures the loop safely iterates zero times when no keys are present and aligns with the intended behavior of returning an empty list by default.
steps.side_effects: This is a minimal, backward-compatible change. Unit tests for DumpsysADBArtifact should be run — existing tests parsing normal dumpsys output should be unaffected. Code that previously relied on parsed['user_keys'] being None (instead of an empty list) will now see an empty list; if any callers distinguish between None and [] this may change behavior and should be reviewed. No external API changes are expected, but add/update tests to assert that when no user_keys are present the parser returns user_keys: [] or equivalent in results to prevent regressions.
